components.title: Attaching
strengths: Symfony 8 and Doctrine ORM 3 stack declared
Static analysis (phpstan) and code style (php-cs-fixer) configured
Unit and integration test suites scaffolded
Clean and conventional Symfony structure (controllers, services, entities)
risks: No CI workflow detected to enforce tests/analysis
No git tags/releases found
Composer package name differs from repo/component name (attachment vs attaching)
Requires PHP ^8.4; environments must align
Documentation appears minimal and no Antora page found in repo
phpunit.xml not committed (only dist), risking config drift

components.title: Documentating
strengths: Canonical snapshot/history contracts are present.
This component is wired into the atlas assessment registry.
risks: Dry-run mode does not perform a live AI verdict.
Repository scan depth is limited to deterministic probes until OPENAI_API_KEY is provided.

components.title: Tagging
strengths: Clear canonical tagging product identity and HTTP contract surface
Runnable host-minimal runtime with Docker and DB flows
Rich governance/audit scripts and security/RBAC/observability configs
Multi-band test/tooling (PHPUnit, PHPStan, Panther, Playwright) with GitHub Actions present
Comprehensive docs, demos, and fixtures
risks: No git release tags reduce release traceability
Requires PHP ^8.4 which may constrain some environments
Dist-only static analysis/test configs risk drift (phpstan.neon.dist, phpunit.xml.dist)
E2E (Playwright/Panther) presence not clearly wired into CI
